name: No-Bake Pineapple Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: Graham cracker squares (12 for a 10-inch plate)
Grape Nuts cereal (1/3 cup for a 10-inch plate)
Hazelnut, walnut, or canola oil (1 tbsp)
Fruit juice or water, as needed (1 tb)
recipeInstructions:
-------------------------
For the crust:
Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 350°F.
Crumble graham crackers into a bowl and process until crumbs form.
Add cereal, sugar, oil, butter, egg white, and juice or water. Pulse until evenly moistened.
Test with a spoonful; add more juice if needed.
Press into an 8-inch springform pan or 10-inch pie plate using hand or back of metal spoon.
Spread evenly over the pan bottom and press in corners.
Bake for 7 minutes, then cool on wire rack before filling.
For the filling:
Place yogurt in fine-mesh strainer set over bowl; strain for at least 20 minutes.
Discard whey and strain crushed pineapple through a fine-mesh strainer.
Press fruit lightly with back of large spoon to release juice; reserve half in small bowl.
Transfer cottage cheese to strainer, cover with plastic wrap, press firmly to remove excess liquid.
Place cottage cheese and cream cheese in blender or food processor; process until smooth for at least 3 minutes.
Add sugar, vanilla, pineapple extract, and drained yogurt. Pulse until well blended.
Scrape sides, pulse several times.
Add remaining crushed pineapple and pulse just to blend.
Combine reserved pineapple juice and lemon juice in saucepan; sprinkle gelatin over mixture and let sit about 3 minutes.
Stir mixture over low heat just until gelatin is dissolved.
With blender running, add gelatin mixture and pulse to blend.
Pour filling into crust and refrigerate at least 3 hours or overnight before serving. Once firm, cover with plastic wrap.
